SAP Data & Analytics

Unit 1: The Evolution of PMM

Printable Learning Guide

Key Statistics to Remember

72% — CMOs increasing budgets 22% — Efficiency gains (top 6%) 15.2% — Enterprise SW growth $6.15T — Global IT spend 2026

The 10x PMM Model

The "10x PMM" isn't someone who uses AI to do the same tasks 10x faster. It's someone who builds a co-working relationship with AI that fundamentally changes what's possible — adapting to a new funnel, a new buyer, and a new way of working.

Two Modes of PMM Work

Production Work (AI-Accelerated) Strategic Work (Human-Led)
First-draft content Narrative architecture
Basic research Competitive strategy
Template enablement Category creation
Production copy Executive communication
Standard analysis Strategic positioning

AI Impact by Activity (Key Examples)

Activity AI Impact Level
First-draft content 94% HIGH
Basic competitive research 91% HIGH
Sales enablement 88% HIGH
Competitive strategy & positioning 28% LOWER
Narrative architecture 18% HUMAN-LED
Executive communication 9% HUMAN-LED

The New Funnel: Key Adaptations

Stage 1: Awareness

What's changed: Buyers start with AI assistants. Content must be discoverable and summarizable.

Stage 2: Consideration

What's changed: Positioning must survive summarization. Write for two audiences: humans and agents.

Stage 3: Evaluation

What's changed: Agents doing RFI/RFP work, creating shortlists. Sales enablement must be structured and searchable.

Stage 4: Commercialization

What's changed: Shift to consumption-based pricing. Commercial models must be transparent and defensible.

Stage 5: Decision

What's changed: Larger, more informed committees. AI helps develop persona-specific content and journeys.

Stage 6: Adoption & Expansion

What's changed: Customers benchmark continuously. Demonstrate value proactively, not at renewal.

Curriculum Overview

Unit Title Funnel Stage
1The Evolution of PMMFoundation
2Creating Awareness through AI DiscoverabilityAwareness
3Building Positioning that Survives SummarizationConsideration
4Enabling Champions for Technical ValidationEvaluation
5Designing Commercial StrategyCommercialization
6Navigating Multi-Stakeholder DecisionsDecision
7Driving Adoption, Expansion, and AdvocacyAdoption

Key Takeaways

  1. Enterprise marketing is being rewired — AI is the central power source, not a bolt-on tool.
  2. High-volume tasks have highest AI impact — freeing you for strategic work that moves the needle.
  3. The funnel is becoming "agentic" — both humans and AI agents are making buying decisions.
  4. Production + Strategic work — know when to direct AI vs. when to lead with human judgment.
  5. Adapt or fall behind — teams doing 2020-era PMM will lose to those who've evolved.
